message:
chore: sync concurrent changes (Cargo.toml, theme_studio_bench, beads)

- Cargo.toml: Dependency updates
- theme_studio_bench.rs: New theme studio benchmarks
- beads: Issue tracker sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.5.2","title":"Auto UI Height Measurement Pass","description":"## Task
Implement auto UI height measurement pass with caching to avoid repeated cost.

## Scope
- Measure UI content height once per stable layout.
- Cache result and invalidate on layout changes.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: cache hit/miss behavior.
- PTY E2E: resize + content changes with JSONL logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Auto height is stable and does not flicker.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.801083132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:09:59.519103507Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:16.627356049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.801083132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":248,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: fixed inline auto height cache bug (min-height measurements were never cached).  now always caches  and only resets diff/scroll region when effective height changes.  {"id":"bd-1rz0.6","title":"Terminal Capability Strategy for Tear‑Free Output","description":"Codify sync‑output vs fallback behavior and ensure safe redraws across muxes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Terminal Capability Strategy for Tear‑Free Output
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Correctness: add property tests for diff/present invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, no-ghosting on shrink)
- Golden outputs: checksum render outputs for canonical fixtures; ensure isomorphism under optimization

 {"id":"bd-1rzr","title":"Action Timeline: Alert Threshold Calibration (Conformal + e-process)","description":"## Goal
Set change-point alert thresholds with formal false-positive control and provide an explanation ledger per alert.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Conformal thresholding on residuals from the sketch baseline:
- Given calibration residuals R = {r_1..r_n}, threshold q = quantile_{1-alpha} using (n+1) rule.
- Alert when r_t > q.

Anytime-valid layer:
- Maintain an e-process on residuals to allow early alerts without inflating false positives.
- Example: e_t = exp(lambda * (r_t - mu) - (lambda^2 * sigma^2)/2), E_t = product e_t.
- Trigger if E_t > 1/alpha.

## Acceptance Criteria
- FPR <= alpha on synthetic and recorded traces.
- Alert carries evidence ledger: residual, q, conformal score, e-value, alpha.
- No alert spam under steady-state traffic.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_conformal_threshold: threshold equals (1-alpha) quantile of calibration set.
- unit_evalue_monotone: e-process nonnegative and grows with evidence.
- property_fpr_control: empirical FPR <= alpha + tolerance.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Replay trace with known change points; log alert timing and evidence.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:09:26.069837634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:27:32.108202783Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T06:27:32.108166835Z","close_reason":"Implemented conformal_alert.rs in ftui-runtime with:
- Conformal thresholding using (n+1) quantile rule
- E-process layer for anytime-valid FPR control
- Evidence ledger with full provenance (residual, q, e-value, alpha)
- 27 passing unit tests including FPR property tests
- GRAPA adaptive lambda support","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-l8x9.1.3","title":"API Spec Subtask: FxQuality + Mapping From DegradationLevel","description":"## Goal
Define `FxQuality` and the explicit mapping from `ftui_render::budget::DegradationLevel` so decorative backdrops degrade gracefully without surprises.

## Scope

1) `FxQuality` levels (recommended)
- `Full`: normal detail
- `Reduced`: fewer iterations / simplified math
- `Minimal`: very cheap fallback (lower frequency, fewer trig ops)
- `Off`: render nothing (decorative)

2) Mapping from `DegradationLevel`
- `Full` -> `Full`
- `SimpleBorders` / `NoStyling` -> `Reduced` (or `Minimal` for expensive FX)
- `EssentialOnly` / `Skeleton` / `SkipFrame` -> `Off`

3) Area-based overrides
- On huge areas (e.g., 240x80), allow clamping `Full -> Reduced` even if budget is `Full`.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests cover every `DegradationLevel` variant.
- Unit tests cover area-based override boundaries (document the threshold constants).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mapping is explicit, stable, and documented in code (table + function).
- Backdrops can become `Off` at low budgets (never blocks essential UI).","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- `FxQuality` variants are final and used across metaballs/plasma/backdrop widget.
- `DegradationLevel -> FxQuality` mapping exists and is unit tested.
- Thresholds for size-based clamping (if any) are constants with tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:32:01.442337219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:04:30.077047670Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:04:17.670891085Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:32:01.442337219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:42.392598591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":170,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context

FX backdrops are decorative. They must degrade gracefully when:
- terminal is huge
- CPU is under load
- runtime budget degrades frame quality (`DegradationLevel`)

## Proposed Quality Levels

```rust
pub enum FxQuality {
    Full,        // full iterations/detail
    Reduced,     // fewer iterations / lower frequency
    Minimal,     // very cheap fallback
    Off,         // render nothing
}
```

## Proposed Mapping

- `DegradationLevel::Full` -> `FxQuality::Full`
- `SimpleBorders` -> `FxQuality::Reduced`
- `NoStyling` -> `FxQuality::Reduced` (or `Minimal` depending on effect cost)
- `EssentialOnly` -> `FxQuality::Off` (background is non-essential)
- `Skeleton` -> `FxQuality::Off`
- `SkipFrame` -> `FxQuality::Off`

## Effect-Specific Overrides

Some effects may choose:
- `Reduced` -> `Minimal` on very large areas
- `Minimal` -> simple static gradient using theme accents

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Mapping is explicit and documented.
- [ ] No surprises: decorative effects never block essential UI.
- [ ] Effects remain visually pleasing even at Reduced/Minimal.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:32:09Z"},{"id":260,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Complete (WhiteMarsh)

### Changes Made:
1. **FxQuality enum** (crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx.rs):
   - Renamed variants from Low/Medium/High to Off/Minimal/Reduced/Full
   - Added Default derive with Full as default
   - Added is_enabled() method for Off checking

2. **DegradationLevel -> FxQuality mapping**:
   - from_degradation(level) - direct mapping
   - from_degradation_with_area(level, cells) - with area-based clamping
   - clamp_for_area(quality, cells) - standalone area clamping

3. **Area threshold constants**:
   - FX_AREA_THRESHOLD_FULL_TO_REDUCED = 16,000 cells (~200x80)
   - FX_AREA_THRESHOLD_REDUCED_TO_MINIMAL = 64,000 cells (~320x200)

4. **Updated effects**:
   - PlasmaFx: early return on Off, uses Minimal for simplified waves
   - MetaballsFx: early return on Off, ball count scaled by quality

5. **Unit tests**: 17 new tests covering all mapping variants and area thresholds

All 70 visual_fx tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:04:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","title":"Implement visual_fx Module Skeleton + Core Types (BackdropFx/FxQuality/ThemeInputs)","description":"## Goal
Create the concrete code-level API surface for `ftui-extras` visual FX so downstream tasks can implement against stable types.

This is the "module + core types" step that unlocks parallel work on:
- Backdrop widget (`bd-l8x9.2.*`)
- Metaballs/Plasma extraction (`bd-l8x9.3.*`, `bd-l8x9.4.*`)
- Theme conversions + quality mapping (`bd-l8x9.1.2`, `bd-l8x9.1.3`)

## Scope
- Add `crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/` module behind the `visual-fx` feature.
- Define the core public types:
  - `BackdropFx` trait (renders into caller-owned `&mut [PackedRgba]`)
  - `FxQuality` enum
  - `ThemeInputs` struct (type only; conversions are separate)
- Doc comments that state invariants:
  - background-only intent
  - determinism expectations
  - tiny-area safety
  - no per-frame allocations required by the trait

## Tests / Validation
- Minimal smoke tests in `ftui-extras` proving the types compile, derive, and basic invariants hold.
- Compile matrix is handled by bd-l8x9.8.4 once features exist.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Module path + Cargo features match `bd-l8x9.1.1`.
- [ ] Types exist and are documented well enough for downstream tasks to implement against.
- [ ] CPU-only builds remain lean (no GPU deps pulled unless explicitly enabled).","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Module path + Cargo features match the scheme in bd-l8x9.1.1.
- Downstream tasks can implement against these types without API thrash.
- `BackdropFx` requires no per-frame allocations (caller-owned buffers).
- CPU-only builds remain lean (no GPU deps unless `fx-gpu` is enabled).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:26.312075147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:19:25.920663114Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T00:19:25.920642295Z","close_reason":"Added ftui_extras::visual_fx module + core types + smoke tests (feature-gated by visual-fx)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","extras","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:26.312075147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:26.312075147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":219,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why This Exists (Plan Optimization)

We want maximum parallelism without API thrash.

This task creates the concrete code-level API surface early (module + types), so:
- effects (`bd-l8x9.3.*`, `bd-l8x9.4.*`) can be implemented in parallel
- the Backdrop widget (`bd-l8x9.2.*`) can be implemented in parallel
- theme conversions (`bd-l8x9.1.2`) and quality mapping (`bd-l8x9.1.3`) can land incrementally

## Scope

Create (feature-gated) `ftui-extras` module + core types:
- `BackdropFx` trait: render into caller-owned `&mut [PackedRgba]`
- `FxQuality` enum
- `ThemeInputs` struct (type definition only)
- any small shared "render context" structs needed (e.g., cell dims/time) as long as:
  - they are cheap to pass
  - they do not allocate
  - they keep the API stable

## Non-Goals

- no widget implementation here (that is `bd-l8x9.2`)
- no theme conversion impls here (that is `bd-l8x9.1.2`)
- no degradation mapping function here (that is `bd-l8x9.1.3`)

## Acceptance

- [ ] module path + Cargo features match `bd-l8x9.1.1`
- [ ] types exist and are documented well enough for downstream tasks to implement against
- [ ] CPU-only builds remain lean (no GPU deps pulled)","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:15:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.10","title":"Docs: Visual FX Backdrops (Composability, Legibility, Performance)","description":"## Task
Document visual FX backdrops: composition model, legibility practices, and performance expectations.

## Scope
- How to enable FX features and select presets.
- Scrim/contrast guidance and accessibility notes.
- Performance budgets and tips for low-power terminals.

## Validation
- Doc review checklist to ensure examples compile.
- Include a small troubleshooting section for flicker/slow output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docs give clear guidance for safe/legible usage.
- All sample code compiles under default features.

## Tests / Validation
- Doc tests for code examples.
- Run FX E2E scripts in doc CI to ensure examples remain valid.
- Include a JSONL logging schema snippet for users in the doc.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:05.652763931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:26:39.912192947Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:05.652763931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:10.536709288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:10.704575952Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":189,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Doc outline:

- Performance policy: quality mapping + size-based degradation
- Example: markdown over metaballs

Acceptance:

-{"id":"bd-l8x9.11","title":"GPU Backend Spike: wgpu Compute for FX (Silent Fallback to CPU)","description":"## Goal
Decide whether a `wgpu` compute backend is worth it for FX generation, while guaranteeing **silent CPU fallback** on any failure.

## Scope
- Prototype a minimal compute pipeline that generates a `w*h` RGBA8 buffer for *one* effect (metaballs first).
- Measure overhead vs CPU at realistic sizes (e.g., 120x40, 240x80).
- Identify the real risks:
  - init latency
  - readback cost
  - device lost / transient failures
  - determinism differences

## Non-Negotiables
- No panics in normal use.
- No user-visible errors/noise: fallback must be silent.
- If GPU init or dispatch fails once, permanently disable GPU for the process.

## Validation
- Compile-check: `cargo check -p ftui-extras --features fx-gpu,visual-fx`.
- Parity sanity: compare CPU vs GPU for a small set of sample points / small buffers within a tolerance (do **not** require bit-identical output).
- Failure path: test-only hook (env var) to force GPU init failure and assert CPU path still renders.

## Deliverable
A short conclusion:
- go/no-go for GPU backend
- expected speedups and where it helps
- proposed API shape for backend selection + fallback guarantees

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GPU backend is fully optional and cannot break CPU builds or runtime.
- [ ] Any init/dispatch failure triggers silent CPU fallback and permanently disables GPU for the process.
- [ ] Measurements compare CPU vs GPU on realistic sizes and include overhead (init + dispatch + readback).
- [ ] Tests cover failure injection and verify CPU correctness under forced GPU failure.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:25.396123590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T14:30:03.783269339Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:25.396123590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:35.437835147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:35.603246766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":190,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.11","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Optional acceleration only: use GPU *if available* to speed up heavy FX computations, but never break CPU builds or runtime.

## Hard Requirements

- Must never panic in normal use.
- Must never surface runtime errors to users (silent fallback).
- If GPU init fails once, disable GPU for the rest of the process.
- Output must remain “good enough” and stable; snapshot tests should remain CPU-based.

## Likely Approach

- Add `fx-gpu` feature in `ftui-extras`.
- Use `wgpu` compute pipeline:
  - inputs: time, params, theme inputs, target dims
  - output: `w*h` RGBA8 buffer
- On startup/first use:
  - try adapter/device init
  - if fails: cache `GpuUnavailable` and always use CPU

## Determinism Concerns

GPU floating point and driver differences can cause subtle output differences.
Policy:
- GPU output must be visually correct, but we should not rely on bit-identical output.
- Any tests should be CPU-only or tolerant.

## Deliverable

A short written conclusion + a minimal proof-of-concept implementation plan:
- whether it is worth it
- what the public API looks like
- how we guarantee silent fallback
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:45Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-l8x9.12","title":"Implement fx-gpu Feature: GPU-Accelerated MetaballsFx (Auto Backend)","description":"## Goal
Implement the `fx-gpu` feature and a GPU-accelerated metaballs backend that is **100% optional** and **silently falls back to CPU**.

## Scope
- `fx-gpu` feature flag in `ftui-extras` with `wgpu` fully behind the gate.
- Backend selection API (internal or public):
  - `Auto` (default when `fx-gpu` is enabled)
  - `CpuOnly` (explicit override for testing/debug)
- Lazy init + cached decision:
  - init once
  - if init fails, permanently disable GPU for the process
  - if dispatch fails (device lost, etc.), permanently disable GPU and continue on CPU

## Testing / Validation
- Compile matrix (via bd-l8x9.8.4): ensure `fx-gpu` builds alongside `visual-fx`.
- Fallback test (bd-l8x9.13): force GPU init failure and assert:
  - no panic
  - no stderr noise
  - CPU output still renders with correct dimensions
- Parity sanity: tolerance-based comparison against CPU at a few sample points / tiny buffers (no full-frame snapshot requirement).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Opt-in only: default builds are unchanged.
- GPU failure never crashes and never prints user-visible errors.
- CPU path remains the reference implementation for determinism tests.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:52.592283277Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:24:36.946142792Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.12","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:52.592283277Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.12","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:58.449393940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.12","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:38:58.614061858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.12","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GPU path is entirely behind `fx-gpu` feature.
- [ ] Runtime selection:
  - default = Auto
  - if GPU init fails, permanently fall back to CPU
  - no panics, no user-visible errors
- [ ] CPU path remains the reference implementation.

## Testing

- CPU tests continue to validate determinism.
- GPU tests (if any) should validate:
  - “does not panic”
  - “produces output of correct shape”
  - fallback triggers correctly when device is unavailable
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:39:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.13","title":"GPU Fallback Validation: Force-Disable GPU and Ensure Silent CPU Path","description":"## Task
Validate GPU fallback behavior by forcing GPU disablement and verifying CPU output/logging.

## Scope
- Env flag or config to force-disable GPU.
- Verify CPU path uses identical seeds/quality settings.

## Tests / Validation
- E2E: run with GPU disabled and compare snapshot output vs CPU baseline.
- Log check: JSONL should record backend=cpu and fallback_reason.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fallback is silent and correct.
- Outputs match CPU baseline within tolerance.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:39:09.410504230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:16:05.035250787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.13","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:39:09.410504230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.13","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:39:14.855972969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":192,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.13","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Ideas:
- Provide an env var (test-only) to force GPU init failure.
- Ensure `FxBackend::Auto` returns CPU backend without error.
- Ensure rendering still produces output of correct dimensions.

Acceptance:
- [ ] no panics
- [ ] no stderr noise
- [ ] CPU path continues to work in all environments
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:39:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.14","title":"GPU Heuristics: When To Prefer CPU vs GPU (Size Thresholds, Power Use)","description":"## Goal
Define conservative heuristics for when to prefer CPU vs GPU for FX rendering.

## Scope
- Consider:
  - render area (`w*h`)
  - `FxQuality` (Full/Reduced/Minimal)
  - whether the GPU backend is already initialized
  - expected readback/dispatch overhead
- Default policy: **prefer CPU unless GPU clearly helps**.

## Debug/Testing Overrides
- Allow a test-only/debug override to force CPU (and optionally force GPU) without affecting default user behavior.
- Default runtime must remain silent (no logs/no stderr noise).

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for decision boundaries (size thresholds, quality thresholds).
- Unit tests for determinism: same inputs -> same decision.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Heuristics are deterministic, documented, and conservative.
- Users never see errors or warnings due to backend selection.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:39:24.866771879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:24:48.143634012Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.14","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:39:24.866771879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.14","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:39:29.001957898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":193,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.14","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Considerations:
- GPU init and readback can cost more than CPU for small buffers.
- Prefer CPU by default unless:
  - terminal area is very large
  - effect complexity is high
  - GPU device already initialized

Acceptance:
- [ ] heuristics are conservative and documented
- [ ] no surprises: user never experiences errors
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:39:35Z"}]}
